title: Paper 70 — Cosmology 4: Large-Scale Structure

d_claims.claim: Check matter power spectrum growth from primordial to late time.
Validate BAO standard ruler distance measurements.
Confirm redshift-space distortion growth rate f sigma_8.

i_claims.claim: CodeLib verifier completeness for Paper 70 is inferred from available stubs
i_claims.reasoning: Verifier coverage is partial or stub-based

x_claims.claim: OBL-70.1 (SM mapping file missing).** Status: **Open.** The file `SM_MAPPING_FLCR-70.md` does not exist. A mapping from SM particle content to the LSS power spectrum or lattice code chain must be constructed before the SM mapping row can be validated.
x_claims.obligation: Open obligation pending external calibration or proof completion

x_claims.claim: OBL-70.2 (E6 cluster map).** Status: **Open.** An explicit map from the 72 E6 roots to the 72 most massive galaxy clusters is not yet derived. The correspondence in Corollary 4.2 is a structural prediction awaiting an explicit cluster catalogue.
x_claims.obligation: Open obligation pending external calibration or proof completion

open_obligations: OBL-70.1 (SM mapping file missing).** Status: **Open.** The file `SM_MAPPING_FLCR-70.md` does not exist. A mapping from SM particle content to the LSS power spectrum or lattice code chain must be constructed before the SM mapping row can be validated.

OBL-70.2 (E6 cluster map).** Status: **Open.** An explicit map from the 72 E6 roots to the 72 most massive galaxy clusters is not yet derived. The correspondence in Corollary 4.2 is a structural prediction awaiting an explicit cluster catalogue.

OBL-70.3 (BAO scale from lattice code).** Status: **Open.** An explicit derivation of the BAO scale $r_{\text{BAO}} \approx 147$ Mpc from the lattice code chain (e.g., from the 24 BAO bins or the E6 glue) is not yet given.

OBL-70.4 (Lattice code chain → LSS formation).** Status: **Open.** The explicit model for LSS formation (e.g., a simulation or analytic construction) from the lattice code chain is not yet constructed. Theorem 4.1 and Corollary 4.3 assert the model but do not provide its implementation.

OBL-70.5 (Gravitational instability as boundary repair proof).** Status: **Open.** The explicit proof that gravitational instability is boundary repair — i.e., that the equations of gravitational collapse map onto the boundary repair operator of Paper 5, Theorem 2.1 — is not yet given. Theorem 3.1, Corollary 3.3, and Theorem 5.1 assert this mapping as interpretation.
